# Questions in Livestream 217

Live Stream #217: Cringe Scholars Society... with Red Wine

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=643" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:43</a> | Which decade has been the best in your experience, and which has been the worst? | He says the early 2000s were probably his best period, while the 1990s were pretty miserable for him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=786" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:06</a> | Is there a science-fiction work that especially influenced your writing? | Yes. He names Babylon 5 as especially influential, while also mentioning earlier influences such as Star Trek and Doctor Who. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=1022" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:02</a> | How did Doctor Who inspire you to pursue higher education? | He says it stirred his imagination and intellectual curiosity in a way that helped push him toward serious study. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=1136" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:56</a> | Based on Scott Stripling's Jericho comments, is he basically adopting Brian Windle's view? | Yes. He says Stripling is essentially in that camp. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=1451" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:11</a> | Before the chariot, did Egyptians use non-military wheeled vehicles for transportation? | No. He says they did not normally use wheeled transport that way before chariots. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=1897" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:37</a> | As a writer, is it reasonable to feel guilty for what your characters suffer? | Yes, to a point. He says your characters should move you, though you still have to let the story do hard things. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=2096" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:56</a> | How should I understand Leviticus 5:15-19 on trespass? Is it really about sustaining ignorance? | He says the passage is about objective guilt and required restitution, not about preserving ignorance as a virtue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=2476" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:16</a> | Is it improper to picture sin offerings like a barbecue where nobody gets to eat? | He says no: barbecue is not a good analogy because these offerings involve incineration and ash rather than shared cooked meat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=2571" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:51</a> | What is your favorite scholarly commentary series on the Bible, and why? | He does not name one favorite; he likes NICOT/NICNT, Hermeneia, and Word, while stressing that commentaries have to be judged book by book. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=2717" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:17</a> | How did the Hebrews celebrate birthdays, if they celebrated them at all? | He says there is little evidence for normal Israelite birthday celebration, unlike some surrounding cultures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=2802" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:42</a> | Would people in that world even know their exact date of birth? | Yes. He says calendars, ritual cycles, and military conscription made exact dates and ages important enough to track. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=2985" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:45</a> | Did the Jewish calendar make birthdays drift from year to year? | Yes, lunar-solar reckoning could complicate strict anniversary matching. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=3133" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:13</a> | In Psalm 1:6, what does "the way of the righteous" mean? | He says it refers to the whole life-path or mode of conduct of the righteous, set over against the path of the wicked. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=3317" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:17</a> | Should psalms be read separately, or in the context of neighboring passages? | Both. He says individual psalms stand on their own, but the Psalter was also arranged by theme, so context matters. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=3520" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:40</a> | Do you know any good resources on how Jews used the Psalms liturgically or from festival to festival? | He recalls that such resources exist, but he cannot confidently name one on the spot. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=3653" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:53</a> | Will there be another book stream? | Yes. He expects another one. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=3772" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:52</a> | Joel 3:4-6 says Phoenicians and Philistines sold Jewish captives to Greeks. What historical period does that fit? | He places it broadly in the first-millennium context where those coastal trading powers and Greek markets overlap. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=4011" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:51</a> | What are your thoughts on the new perspective on Paul? | He finds parts of it useful, but rejects versions that make Paul's Christianity merely a later invention or flatten the gospel into covenant sociology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=4157" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:17</a> | When will the sequel to Reliquary of the Dead be available? | He says it will be available in four days and notes that a third book is also underway. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=4215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:15</a> | Given that Nineveh is landlocked, how long would it take Jonah to get from the coast near Aleppo to Nineveh? | He says about a week, maybe two if delayed, since that route was not especially long by ancient standards. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=4340" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:20</a> | What do you think the fish in Jonah was? | He does not identify a precise species and is content to leave it at a large fish rather than over-specifying it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=4417" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:37</a> | Was Jonah intended as satire or as a real story? | He says it was meant as a real story. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=4471" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:31</a> | Did Jonah die and come back to life after being swallowed? | He leans yes, treating Jonah as having died and then been restored. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=4904" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:44</a> | Any more movement on an audiobook? | Yes, but with a snag. Some chapters were recorded, though the first ones may need re-recording or repair. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=5108" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:08</a> | How much would you need to charge to make audiobook costs back? | He says audiobook production is extremely expensive, with market pricing around twenty dollars and little realistic profit margin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=5244" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:24</a> | Any thoughts on T. C. Smith's Josephus and Jesus? | He has not read it, so he declines to comment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=5294" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:14</a> | What did Jerome mean when he said Jews had excised Tobit from the catalog of divine Scriptures and moved it to the Apocrypha? | He says Jerome was reflecting his preference for the Jewish canon and treating Tobit as excluded from Scripture rather than properly canonical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=5387" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:47</a> | If Tobit could be excised, does that mean it had first been part of Jewish Scripture? | Not necessarily. He says canon formation worked more as a process of elimination from a broad pool of texts than as a simple add-then-remove sequence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=5758" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:58</a> | How reliable is Papias? | It depends on the topic. He says Papias is early and interesting, but uneven like other church fathers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=5811" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:51</a> | What do you think of Scott Stripling's Shiloh dig? | He praises the fieldwork and organization, but says the reporting is often badly biased and distorts what was found. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=5922" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:42</a> | Can we still glean anything useful from ABR's dig even if they distort things? | Some useful artifacts were found, but he says contextual distortion makes the interpretation much harder to trust. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=6165" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:45</a> | After getting a THM, what should I be doing now if I want to teach at a university? | He says to keep building toward a PhD, produce research early, and have backup plans because the field is brutally competitive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=6383" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:23</a> | What is even the process for getting hired into one of those university jobs? | Usually PhD, then postdoc or fellowship, then more publication and networking before competing for a very small number of jobs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=6599" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:59</a> | Should I only go for Bible-college jobs, or secular ones as well? | He says go for both: secular schools care more about research, while Bible colleges care more about doctrine and denominational fit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=6803" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:23</a> | Will the video game be your main focus after the books are done? | Yes, at least for the next couple of months. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=7332" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:12</a> | Do some stories only work in certain media, and how do you choose the best medium? | Yes. He says the right medium depends on the type of story and on the medium the writer is actually equipped to write well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=7616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:56</a> | What do you think of The X-Files? | He likes it as paranormal fiction, though he says it can be disturbing and does not really recommend it universally. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=7979" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:59</a> | How would you explain Genesis 5:24 under your long-ages or clan-style view? | He treats Enoch's case as compatible with that framework, without seeing it as a decisive problem for the view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=8116" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:16</a> | What can we say about the eternal destiny of someone who seemed to be turning toward Jesus but died before making a clear faith commitment? | He says we do not know for certain, but there is at least ground for hope and the matter belongs to the Lord's judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=8297" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:17</a> | Was Terah Abraham's biological father rather than a clan progenitor? | He says it is hard to know for sure and does not think the text forces one answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=8553" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:33</a> | What is your favorite whiskey? | He names his favorite directly and answers as a matter of taste rather than scholarship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=8776" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:16</a> | Have you been to Russia and tried vodka? | He answers personally and says he has not had that experience in the way the question suggests. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=8892" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:12</a> | What is cognac? | He says it is essentially a type of brandy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=9019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:19</a> | I'm visiting Toronto for the first time. Are there any restaurants I should go to? | He gives a few remembered recommendations, while admitting his memory may be partial. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=9466" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:46</a> | Same question, but how should we think about Noah and his sons as clan progenitors? | He says the same clan-progenitor logic can apply there too. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=9610" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:10</a> | Are Joseph and Daniel the only Old Testament figures with no recorded sin? Do you agree? | He does not fully agree: Joseph's pride seems implied in the narrative, while Daniel is less clearly shown that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=9850" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:10</a> | What does the "burning coals" image mean in Romans 12:20? | He explains it with the ancient image of carrying a brazier on the head, tied to repentance, distress, or a plea for help. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=10150" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:10</a> | Have you ever played Pyramid: Challenge of the Pharaoh's Dream, and if so how accurate is it? | No. He never played it, so he cannot assess its historical accuracy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=10245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:45</a> | Do you believe Daniel was a castrated eunuch? | He says Daniel was made a eunuch, but probably not by surgical castration in the usual sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=10352" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:32</a> | How long have you been doing keto? | He answers from personal experience and says it has been several years. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=11332" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:52</a> | Is goat's milk and sheep's milk also a no-go for lactose-intolerant people? | Yes, he says those can still be a problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=11966" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:19:26</a> | What is the second-best Ultima after Exodus? | He picks Ultima: Pagan as his runner-up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=12232" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:52</a> | How excited are you about AI helping read the Herculaneum scrolls? | Cautiously interested. He wants publications and readable data more than publicity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=12370" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:10</a> | Are Solomon's 700 wives and 300 concubines literal, symbolic, or exaggerated? | He thinks the numbers may be rounded, but still reflect a genuinely huge treaty-marriage system. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=13213" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:13</a> | Is there any update on the Amorite Akkadian cuneiform tablet from 2023, and are there other biblical ramifications? | No major update since publication. He says its main significance is how strongly it supports Amoritic as Hebrew's closest cognate and thus illuminates Israel's Amorite background. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=13450" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:10</a> | What do you think of the idea that only Levites came from outside Israel while the other tribes were Canaanites? | He rejects that small-exodus view and says it depends too heavily on documentary-hypothesis assumptions while failing to explain the conquest adequately. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=14098" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:58</a> | Do you have advice on presenting scholarly work so lay people can understand it? | Yes: translate jargon, use simple words and sentences, and write at a very accessible reading level. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=14872" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:52</a> | What do you think about the Manoppello image and its match to the shroud? | He sees it as one devotional artifact among several similar Christ-image traditions, shaped by familiar Byzantine iconographic patterns rather than as unique proof of authenticity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=15057" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:10:57</a> | Why are apologists usually worse at defending the Old Testament than the New Testament? | He says the Old Testament takes far more background knowledge, languages, and history to master, so many apologists never develop the depth it requires. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4fDBgW37xto?t=15453" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:17:33</a> | Is it possible to write a horror story with a good Christian message? | Yes. He says horror can carry Christian themes well when the world, contrast, and moral stakes are built carefully. |
